answer.text: The total risk of the stock
The systematic or market risk of the stock relative to the overall market
The dividend yield of the stock
The PE ratio relative to the market PE

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Beta measures systematic (market) risk. Beta = 1 means moves with market. Beta > 1 = more volatile. Beta < 1 = less volatile than market. **The Trap:** Beta measures systematic risk only, not total risk (which includes unsystematic risk captured by SD). **Cross-Exam Context:** Tested in NISM XV Ch 12. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Defensive stocks (pharma, FMCG) typically have beta < 1. Cyclical stocks (metals, auto) have beta > 1.
